title: Claude Code /ultrareview Deep Dive — Cloud Bug-Hunting Agents Before Every Merge
whyTrending: Shipped early May 2026 as research preview (CLI 2.1.86). Fleet of 5–20 cloud agents, each focused on different bug class (concurrency, SQL injection, error boundaries). Every finding independently reproduced before reporting. $5–$20/run, 5–10 min. No competitor blog post covers the practical workflow yet.

title: MCP RCE Vulnerability Exposed 200K Servers — What the Anthropic 'Working as Designed' Response Means for Your Stack
whyTrending: Ox Security disclosure on April 15, 2026: STDIO transport allows arbitrary OS command execution before subprocess handshake. 200+ projects, 150M downloads, 7K+ public servers, 4 exploitation families incl. zero-click prompt injection in Cursor/Windsurf. Anthropic declined to patch — calls it 'expected behavior'. Banking sector flagged. Massive ongoing controversy.

title: Anthropic Acquired Bun — What This Means for Claude Code, JS Tooling, and Your Stack in 2026
whyTrending: December 2025 acquisition. Bun now core infrastructure for Claude Code. Still MIT/open-source, but corporate alignment with the leading AI coding tool changes the calculus for Node.js teams. Bun 1.3 at ~98% npm compat, 2–3.5x HTTP throughput, 4–10x faster cold starts, 20–40x faster installs. The strategic angle nobody else is writing.

title: AGENTS.md vs CLAUDE.md — The Open Standard 60K Repos Already Adopted (And When to Use Each)
whyTrending: AGENTS.md governed by Agentic AI Foundation under Linux Foundation since Dec 2025. Used by OpenAI Codex, Cursor, Amp, Google Jules, Factory. Next.js 16.2 added it to create-next-app default. 60K+ repos. Pragmatic angle: do you write CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or both — and how do they overlap with skills/MCP?

title: MCP Apps — Build Rich HTML Interfaces Inside Claude (The First Official MCP Extension)
whyTrending: Shipped January 26, 2026 as the FIRST official extension to the MCP spec. Tools return sandboxed iframes with bidirectional JSON-RPC between host and UI. Turns MCP from data plumbing into a UI surface — agents can now show charts, forms, dashboards inline. Practical guide gap: zero good tutorials exist.

title: Spec-Driven Development with Spec Kit + Claude Code — Replacing PRDs with Executable Specs
whyTrending: GitHub Spec Kit, Kiro, OpenSpec, Tessl, BMAD-METHOD all surged in early 2026. Thoughtworks called SDD one of 2025's defining engineering practices. Constitutional SDD paper (arXiv Feb 2026) formalizes spec-as-prompt with CWE security mappings. Concrete workflow guide is missing.

title: The Composable AI Coding Stack — Why You'll Use Cursor, Claude Code, AND Codex Together in 2026
whyTrending: Pragmatic Engineer survey (Feb 2026, n=906): 95% weekly AI use, 75% half their work, 55% regularly use agents. Five tools own production: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex Desktop, Replit Agent 3, Devin. The narrative shifted from 'pick one' to 'compose orchestration + execution + review layers'. Rebuts the existing comparison-style posts.

title: Agent Observability in Production — Langfuse vs Arize Phoenix vs Helicone (Pick One)
whyTrending: Major gap in the blog: agents shipped, but how do you watch them? ClickHouse acquired Langfuse Jan 2026. Six platforms now own production: LangSmith, Langfuse, Arize Phoenix, Helicone, Datadog LLM, Honeycomb LLM. OpenTelemetry standardization, eval primitives, drift detection. Decision-framework guide gap.

title: Next.js 16.2 — AGENTS.md, Browser Log Forwarding, and next-browser Explained
whyTrending: Next.js 16.2 shipped recently (16.2.4 on April 16, 2026, 16.3 canary running). 200+ Turbopack fixes, ~400% faster dev startup, ~50% faster SSR. AGENTS.md added to create-next-app default. Browser log forwarding for AI debugging. next-browser experimental. Practical migration guide overdue.

title: Vercel AI SDK 5 → 6 — Building Streaming Agents with stopWhen, Agent class, and SSE
whyTrending: AI SDK 5 (Jul 2025) introduced SSE-only streaming and Agent class with stopWhen primitives. AI SDK 6 followed with provider-executed functions, dynamic tools, lifecycle hooks. Most React/Next devs in audience already use AI SDK; gap is production agent patterns specifically for the 5→6 transition.
